prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response:
Oh, sweetheart, think of the computer like your busy little toy kitchen. The CPU is the special spot right in the middle—like your own two hands.

That’s where all the real “cooking” happens. It’s the part that reads the recipe, mixes the pretend cake batter, and tells all the other parts what to do, very, very quickly.

All the pictures, the words, and even the little sounds come from it following millions of those tiny instructions. So it’s really the tiny, clever heart of the whole machine, working away to make all your games and stories come to life.
